source_citation: World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database - International Telecommunication Union (ITU), via World Bank (2026). OurWorldinData.org/internet | CC BY

y_axis.tick_format_note: Each y-tick label is printed as 'N%' (0%, 20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, 100%). Parsed by stripping the trailing '%' to recover the numeric percentage; recorded values are in percent units (0-100), not fraction (0-1).

chart_title: Share of the population using the Internet
chart_subtitle: Share of the population who used the Internet in the last three months.
notes: Our World in Data chart. Multi-series line plot with point markers at each integer year (2005-2025), 8 regional series. Legend labels are placed at each line's right endpoint (no legend box). The y-axis prints as percent ('0%'-'100%'); values in data.csv are in percent units. Two points are linear interpolations because the marker is fully occluded by an overlapping series at that year: Latin America and Caribbean (WB) @ 2020 (occluded by East Asia and Pacific (WB) which sits at the same y), and Sub-Saharan Africa (WB) @ 2005 and 2006 (occluded by South Asia (WB) near 2%). See interpolation_notes.json.
